name: Palau Guell
summary: A short, central Gaudi stop that is easier to fit than the bigger blockbuster sites.
whyItMatters: It gives the trip a UNESCO/Gaudi hit without committing half a day, and it is relatively close to central hotels, La Rambla, and Poble-sec/Montjuic approaches.
pros: UNESCO-linked Gaudi architecture in a compact format
Central location can pair with a meal or walk rather than a dedicated museum day
Potentially easier than Sagrada Familia or Park Guell on a race-heavy schedule
cons: Still needs advance ticket planning in summer
Not directly on the Montjuic route; may be awkward during race closures

logistics.opening_hours: Official site should be checked for July 2026 hours and free/closed days.
logistics.distance_from_base: Central Barcelona base dependent; near La Rambla / El Raval.
logistics.distance_from_viewing_point: Roughly 2 km from Montjuic Park, depending on exact viewing point.
logistics.transit: Metro/bus/walk from central Barcelona; verify race-day street closures.
logistics.road_closure_notes: Potential central Barcelona and Montjuic access impacts on July 4-5, 2026.

timing.best_visit_window: A Barcelona non-race evening/morning, or Stage 1 morning if central access is calm.

nearbyFoodDrinkPairing: Pair with El Raval or Poble-sec tapas; nearby La Boqueria works as a quick food stop if crowds are acceptable.

sources.title: Palau Guell - Wikipedia
sources.note: Secondary support for Gaudi authorship, UNESCO World Heritage context, location, and compact-house description.

open_questions: Are timed tickets available for the exact Barcelona dates once the trip schedule is fixed?
Would this duplicate other planned Gaudi stops?
